items.title: Link 1
items.reason_ja: ★★★☆☆ Superintelligence誌（Kim Isenberg）の「Compute-to-Cash Race」— Anthropic対OpenAIの収益比較・IPO準備論。本文でAnthropicのrun-rate算出方法（過去28日の消費収益×13＋定期収益×12）とOpenAIとの会計方法の違い（総収益vs純収益）を指摘。ただし Anthropic収益データ($47B run-rate, $65B Series H)は既にentities/anthropic.mdに収録済み。OpenAI IPO情報もentities/sam-altman.md等でカバー。新規概念ページ作成には至らないが、既存ページの「収益比較の会计学的不透明性」セクションとして参照価値あり。
items.body_excerpt: Anthropic's revenue run rate has overtaken OpenAI's on paper. But when you look beneath the headlines, the real question is not who is bigger, but who converts scarce compute into durable, high-margin enterprise revenue more efficiently... Anthropic calculates its run rate by taking the last 28 days of consumption-based revenue, multiplying by 13, then adding subscription revenue multiplied by 12 (Reuters Breakingviews, 03/10/2026). That formula means any short-term spike in token consumption... gets annualized into an eye-catching headline number. It is a legitimate accounting approach, but it can overstate what a stable, repeatable annual revenue figure would look like.
